one_time_description: Nation separated from Tl'azt'en Nation effective April 1, 2019; net assets of $4,148,031 transferred including buildings, water/sewage system, vehicles, receivable from Tl'azt'en Nation, and debt
economic_notes: Nation is economically dependent on Indigenous Services Canada and other government agencies. Portfolio investment consists of limited partnership units in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing Limited Partnership. Nation was newly separated from Tl'azt'en Nation effective April 1, 2019.
notes: This is the first year of operations for Binche Whut'en following separation from Tl'azt'en Nation effective April 1, 2019. Audit is qualified due to: (1) inability to verify housing arrears and Ottawa Trust Fund allocations from Tl'azt'en Nation; (2) no budget was prepared for the year as required by PSAB. All 2019 comparative figures are zero except opening accumulated surplus of $4,148,031 transferred from Tl'azt'en Nation. Receivable from Tl'azt'en Nation of $1,392,493 is unsecured and has no fixed repayment terms. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
